methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Type One Energy

data.narrative:
Thea Energy is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Brian Berzin that has raised $137M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector comp universe comprises 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo, though a sufficiently narrow peer set at identical stage and valuation tier does not exist for reliable median benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable triangulation method, uses the company's $100M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $190M. The method is grounded in observed capital step-ups between funding rounds but lacks validation from a tight peer cohort; the nuclear energy sector's limited same-stage private data (only 1 comparable at identical stage) prevented peer-median calibration.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, centered on $190M. Key uncertainties stem from the reliance on a single triangulation method due to sparse nuclear energy private market comparables, and the inherent volatility in applying historical step-up multiples to an early-stage capital-intensive sector. Refinement would require either additional same-stage nuclear peers or downstream funding events to validate the stage-transition multiple.
